answer:
\boxed{Rangoon (Yangon), Burma (Myanmar)}

Bahadur Shah II was exiled to **Rangoon (now Yangon)** in Burma (now Myanmar) in 1858 after the British crushed the Sepoy Mutiny. The British authorities, fearing his influence, sent him into exile to isolate him from Indian political circles. This decision marked the end of Mughal rule in India.
